name: kimiflow
description: Context-aware Kimiflow launcher, feature and bug-fix loop for Codex with mechanical quality gates, project intelligence maps, token-cheap memory/learning recall, Obsidian Terminal MCP setup, health/auth status, and Vault sync handoffs.

interface.shortDescription: Understand a codebase, plan changes, and run feature/fix work through gates
interface.longDescription: Kimiflow opens with a context-aware launcher when invoked without a concrete task, then routes the user into project mapping, memory/recall curation, Obsidian auto-detect/connect, Terminal-based Obsidian MCP setup, Vault health/auth checks, Vault prefetch/sync handoffs, findings, backlog resume, feature work, bug fixes, improvement analysis, or docs. The Terminal wizard lets the user enter the Obsidian API key outside chat, writes host config, and keeps secrets out of .kimiflow/. It also runs a disciplined feature and bug-fix loop in Codex, and can build a local project intelligence map before coding. It maps the codebase, architecture, conventions, tests, flows, stale sections, docs, opt-in improvement/refactoring ideas, and bounded project memory under .kimiflow/project/. Normal coding runs use that map and memory recall to understand what already exists before planning, then continue through mechanical plan, verification, review, and commit gates.
defaultPrompt: Open kimiflow and show project status
Map and understand this codebase with kimiflow
Refresh kimiflow project memory and recall
Open Obsidian MCP Terminal setup with kimiflow
Connect and check Obsidian memory health with kimiflow
Find architecture and refactoring opportunities with kimiflow
